title: Warning Types
text: Warning Types There are seven different $LICENSE_OPTIONS warning types: Note: The default number of days for providing alerts for each notification type is different. All warning types options accepts On and Off, true and false. On/true defaults to 30 days, Off/false is the same as 0. Warning type Setting Description License It does not have a setting. It is covered under LM_NOTIFICATION and LM_TRANSCRIPT . The setting LM_NOTIFICATION can be turned off for all license notification settings in one go. For example : LM_NOTIFICATION=0|Off|No Copy This will turn off all license related notification types, such as postmessage, transcript, logfile, and msgbox notifications. License Expiry Notifications 30 days before a license expires, Uniface starts sending the following warning messages about the upcoming expiration: License for feature FeatureName will expire in nn days. License for feature FeatureName expires today. By default, these messages are displayed in a dialog box and transcript window (in a desktop application), or in the application's log file. You can set the length of the notification period for the notification message and the transcript window, or even suppress these messages, using the LM_NOTIFICATION and LM_TRANSCRIPT options. For example: $LICENSE_OPTIONS=LM_LICENSE_FILE=D:/MyLicenses/myUnifaceLicense.xml, LM_NOTIFICATION=15, LM_TRANSCRIPT=15 Copy If set to 0 , no warning messages are sent. You can customize the messages and the way they are displayed by defining this behavior in the receiveMessage trigger of the application. To instruct Uniface to redirect the notification message to this trigger, set the option LM_NOTIFICATION_POSTMESSAGE . This sends the message as an asynchronous message (postmessage) instead of displaying it in a dialog box. Lease LM_RENEWAL_NOTIFICATION This setting can be turned off or set to a starting point for lease renewal notifications. The default starting points are: Postmessage: 3 days The transcript window: 3 days The log file: 3 days The message box popup: 3 days By default, three days before a Sentinel license lease must be renewed, Uniface starts sending the following warning messages: Connect to the internet within nn days to renew the lease for license feature FeatureName . Connect to the internet today to renew the lease for license feature FeatureName . You can set the length of the notification period, using the LM_RENEWAL_NOTIFICATION . If set to 0 , no warning messages are sent. By default, these messages are displayed in a dialog box. You can customize the messages and the way they are displayed by defining this behavior in the receiveMessage trigger of the application. To instruct Uniface to redirect the notification message to this trigger, set the option LM_NOTIFICATION_POSTMESSAGE . This sends the message as an asynchronous message (postmessage) instead of displaying it in a dialog box. Version LM_UPGRADE_NOTIFICATION This setting can be turned off or set to a starting point for version upgrade notifications. The default starting points are: Postmessage: 14 days The transcript window: 3 days The log file: 3 days The message box popup: 3 days Maintenance support and No more maintenance support LM_MAINTENANCE_NOTIFICATION This setting can be turned off if no more maintenance support is required or set to a starting point for maintenance support notifications. The default starting point settings are OFF for all notification types for maintenance support. Note: The no more maintenance support (LM_MAINTENANCE_NOTIFICATION = OFF) is the only one that applies after the end date has passed. Maintenance Expiry Notifications By default, no notification is send when the maintenance contract is about to expire or has already expired. You can change this behavior by setting LM_MAINTENANCE_NOTIFICATION to the number of days before the maintenance end date arrives that you want to be notified of this event. The messages that can then be send are: The maximum release date for feature USEC will be reached in nn days. The maximum release date for feature USEC has been reached. The maximum release date for feature USEC is in the past. Note: You need to update your license to use patches released after this date. The actual maximum release date appears in the transcript window only when the configuration settings for $ioprint is set to 64. By default, these messages are displayed in a dialog box. You can customize the messages and the way they are displayed by defining this behavior in the receiveMessage trigger of the application. To instruct Uniface to redirect the notification message to this trigger, set the option LM_NOTIFICATION_POSTMESSAGE . This sends the message as an asynchronous message (postmessage) instead of displaying the message in a dialog box. Grace License NA The grace license type does not have a setting. Additionally, it cannot be turned off and its starting point cannot be set either. Commuter License LM_COMMUTER_NOTIFICATION This setting can be turned off or set to a starting point for commuter license notifications. The default starting points are: Postmessage: 14 days The transcript window: 3 days The log file: 3 days The message box popup: 3 days The LM_NOTIFICATION_POSTMESSAGE , LM_TRANSCRIPT and LM_LOGFILE settings control the license warning types activation. The order in which the warning notifications are overridden is explained in the following table, where a lower value (e.g. OFF or 10 days) overrides a higher value (e.g. 30 days): Table 1. Warning notifications order of execution Options Specified Values None of these three ( LM_NOTIFICATION_POSTMESSAGE , LM_TRANSCRIPT and LM_LOGFILE settings) in IDE postmessage=30(!), transcript=OFF, logfile=OFF, msgbox=OFF None of these three ( LM_NOTIFICATION_POSTMESSAGE , LM_TRANSCRIPT and LM_LOGFILE settings) in other Uniface applications postmessage=OFF, transcript=30, logfile=30, msgbox=ON LM_NOTIFICATION_POSTMESSAGE=True|On|Yes postmessage=30, transcript=OFF, logfile=OFF, msgbox=OFF LM_NOTIFICATION_POSTMESSAGE=0|Off|No postmessage=OFF, transcript=30, logfile=30, msgbox=ON LM_NOTIFICATION_POSTMESSAGE=1..100 postmessage=1..100, transcript=OFF, logfile=OFF, msgbox=OFF LM_TRANSCRIPT =True|On|Yes transcript=30, logfile=30(?), msgbox=ON LM_TRANSCRIPT =0|Off|No transcript=OFF, logfile=OFF(?), msgbox=ON LM_TRANSCRIPT =1..100 transcript=1..100, logfile=1..100(?), msgbox=ON LM_LOGFILE =True|On|Yes logfile=30(*), msgbox=ON LM_LOGFILE =True|On|Yes logfile=<transcript-value>, msgbox=ON LM_LOGFILE =0|Off|No logfile=OFF, msgbox=ON LM_LOGFILE =1..100 logfile=1..100(?), msgbox=ON Note: The markings in the above table represents the following: (!) : The postmessaging value cannot be overridden. (?): For compatibility, LM_TRANSCRIPT is used to control both the transcript window and the log file. (*): If transcript is off. Note: The msgbox does not have a setting and applies to Windows only. The msgbox is off only when POSTMESSAGE is used. The 4GL application then handles the warning. Note: The lower value for postmessage, transcript, logfile, and msgbox is determined by a warning type setting, and those determined by the above LM_NOTIFICATION_POSTMESSAGE , LM_TRANSCRIPT and LM_LOGFILE settings will be the effective setting, where OFF is the lowest value. Also note that, 1..100 means any number between 1 and 100. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
